rationale: The citing paper engages Hattula et al. (2015) in detail, summarizing its findings that marketers show false consensus effects and that empathy can increase this bias. It uses a specific limitation of that prior study (no suppression-only condition) to motivate the new research question about whether preference suppression helps or backfires. The citation also informs the paper's conceptual setup and study design continuity, rather than serving as a passing background mention.
relation_description: The 2021 paper directly builds on Hattula et al. (2015) as prior evidence of managerial egocentric prediction and then extends that line by isolating preference suppression effects, adding preference certainty as a moderator, and testing the proposed mechanism/intervention.
